blocks.content: He took something from me that I cannot get back. He made a choice — a deliberate, calculated choice — and because of it, my life bent in a direction it was never supposed to go. I've spent eight years living inside the consequences of what he did and he has spent eight years living as though it never happened.
That's the part that never stopped hurting. Not the thing itself. The fact that he never once flinched.

I've thought about this weekend for a long time. The beach house. The six of us. Two and a half days with nowhere to go, no distractions, no excuse to leave the room when things get uncomfortable. I chose this. I arranged it. Nobody knows that — they all think it was Cade's idea, because I made sure it was Cade's idea. That's the thing about being patient. You learn to work with the geometry of a situation before the situation even exists.

The weekend looked simple enough from the outside. Six old friends, a house on the Carolina coast, the kind of trip we used to take before real life swallowed us all. Nobody will question it. Nobody will look too closely.
